blocks.content: While brushing her teeth in front of the mirror, Amy lifted her short hair.
She didn't dislike it.
She was a soldier and knew how useless appearances were in battle.
But when she returned to everyday life from the battlefield, she was still a woman.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Sigh.
As she finished washing her face and came out, there was a knock on the door.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy, are you in?
It was Shirone's voice.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: She had imagined this countless times, but when it actually happened, she felt a sudden fear.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Just a moment.
After drying her face with a towel, she opened the door to find Shirone with an awkward smile.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What brings you here at this hour?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Come in.
After briefly checking the hallway, Amy closed and locked the door.
Click.
The sound seemed louder than thunder as Amy spoke first.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You came because you were thinking about what happened this morning, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well, Amy…

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's okay. I was upset, but I've come to a good conclusion. I was too sensitive.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: She thought he had a really strong heart.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That's why I like him.
Amy smiled as she turned around.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Thanks for telling me. I thought you didn't care at all, but you were thinking about it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha, of course.
The misunderstanding from the morning was cleared up, but Shirone couldn't bring himself to leave now that he had made up his mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So… should I just go now?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No, since you're here, would you like some tea?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Do you want to sit here for a moment?
As Shirone patted the spot next to him on the bed, Amy finally realized.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why all of a sudden…
As she sat on the bed, Shirone leaned in slightly.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Wait!
Shirone froze as if time had stopped.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: W-why?
Amy got up from the bed, turned off the light, and returned to Shirone's side.
Time resumed for Shirone as he hadn't moved a muscle until she returned.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy…
As they heard rustling in the darkness and their breathing grew heavier…
Great Star!
Someone knocked on the door loudly.
Both of them jumped up simultaneously, and Shirone rolled off the bed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who is it?
As he turned on the light and looked back, Amy was fixing her clothes under the blanket.

blocks.speaker: Principal Nikolai
blocks.content: Great Star! Are you inside?
It was Principal Nikolai's voice.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Just a moment.
When he opened the door, Nikolai stood there with a pale face and sweating profusely.

blocks.speaker: Nikolai
blocks.content: What is it?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: A guest?
A certified class 4 mage wouldn't be shaken by most things.
Knowing this fact, Amy kicked off the blanket and approached the door.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Who is it?
There was an unusual mix of anger in her voice, and Shirone sided with her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well…
Nikolai wiped his forehead with a handkerchief and stepped back.
Only then did Shirone realize.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh!
A promise suddenly came to mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone.
As the door slowly opened and the guest entered, Amy's mouth dropped open in shock.

blocks.speaker: Archangel Ikael
blocks.content: Mom.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Mom? Mom?
The conclusion that quickly formed in Amy's mind hit her like a hammer blow to the back of her head.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What? Mom?
Ikael's eyebrows rose in surprise at the unexpected atmosphere.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I came immediately because I heard it was urgent. Did I interrupt something?

It was because of Wizard's situation. Also, due to the nature of simultaneous events, there couldn't be two Shirones in one place. They had arranged to meet Ikael beforehand and separated in another country.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I didn't expect her to come at night…
After sending Nikolai away, Shirone introduced the two women awkwardly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is Amy. Amy, this is my biological mother…
It felt awkward to say it out loud.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is Ikael.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hello. I'm Amy.
Ikael looked at Amy with gentle eyes as she greeted politely.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: So this is her.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I've heard a lot about you from Shirone. You're very pretty. I'm Ikael.
The reason she didn't say 'biological mother' was because of her lingering guilt towards Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: So what's going on?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well… Archangel Satiel has become the new angel leader and is leading heaven's army. At first, the archangels were scattered, but Rayel seems to have sided with Satiel.
There had been many events within heaven's army as well.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: My mom and I traveled around trying to persuade ordinary angels. But we had no success. It seems every single one decided to follow Satiel.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Every single one?
Ikael explained.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Angels are slightly different from humans. Unanimity is extremely rare in human society, but angels act based on absolute standards. When opinions differ, decisions are made by the archangel council called the White Hall.
Ikael used to be the final decision-maker there.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The White Hall was an institution with supreme authority in heaven. The fact that two members agreed played a significant role. If Uriel sided with my mom, it could shake heaven's army…
Uriel intended to kill Ikael.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmm.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What is Satiel planning? If she united heaven's army, something should have happened by now. But this is my first time hearing about it today.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: We don't know exactly either. At some point, tracking became impossible. We know they're on this planet for sure. According to Rampa's investigation, their trail ends in Jive Kingdom.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmm, Jive. A powerful nation.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Yes. It's likely related to the Holy War Council. The giants and fairies are also showing independent movements. Tormia has many cards to play but can't be complacent given heaven's situation.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Indeed. Fermis side is also suspicious.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: I'm sorry we couldn't help more by persuading angels.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No need to apologize. The angels left because I supported Shirone and humanity. I'm not representing anyone but I'm grateful for that.
Seeing Ikael regain her smile made Amy feel better too.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But… why are you here today?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, I asked her to come because of Wizard. She'll help open temporal perception.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Aha.
Shirone had also learned Ataraxia by resonating his mind with Ikael's before.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But isn't it dangerous? You said one could lose themselves if not careful.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: That's why personal will is important. She'll be fine; she's talented and young which helps in opening senses.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Mom, what's wrong?
Although Shirone had grown much more comfortable over time, there was something about Ikael's presence at moments like this that made him tense.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: It's late, so I'll head back for now. You two should spend a bit more time together. I didn't mean to intrude.
Ikael was one of the few angels who had experienced how humans love.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No, it's fine. I was about to go to bed too. I just called Shirone because I needed to discuss something briefly, right, Shirone?
Clenching her teeth, Amy stared daggers at Shirone, signaling him to get out.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Oh, right. That's what happened. Mom, you should go now. I'll check if there are any empty rooms available.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: No need for that. I'll step out for a bit—I have something to take care of. See you both in the morning.
Since there was no task that Shirone didn't know about, he figured she was just being considerate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Good night. Sleep well, Shirone.
After seeing Shirone and Ikael out, Amy closed the door and leaned against it.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Phew.
At least, she wouldn't be having any unnecessary thoughts for a while.
In the dead of night, while everyone was asleep, blue sparks gathered on the rooftop of the dormitory.
They swirled and took the form of a crouching figure.
Fwooo…
Exhaling deeply, the man, clad in a tight black bodysuit, stood still.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: …This is going to be tricky.
Electricity coursed over the white mask covering his face, forming a neon expression—a childish, cartoonish display as if drawn by a kid.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: But I've been paid, so…
Gunjo Vedium. The top assassin of the Black Line and the father of Gunjo Lycan, who had been killed by Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: Let's test the waters first.
Had Ikael not visited earlier, it would have been the perfect opportunity to kill Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: Amy…
Given how things had turned out, his immediate goal was to rattle Shirone emotionally.
Bzzt!
Once again, Vedium's body dissolved into free electrons, and a wide grin appeared on his mask.
Like sinking into the ground, he vanished, only to reappear in a room filled with a pleasant fragrance.
Amy was sound asleep.
Sensing her brainwaves through the free electrons, Vedium gave a small nod.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: Utterly stable.
In his hand was a blade of crackling electricity shaped like an elongated S.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: No emotions.
If he struck her with this, the current would course through her organs, stopping her heart instantly.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: Life's just like that, isn't it?
Reciting a silent incantation to suppress his killing intent, he plunged the electric blade toward her chest.
Crackle!
At the same time, a surge of electricity erupted from his chest.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: What?
Realizing a fist had pierced through his body, Vedium quickly moved to the corner and lifted his head.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: An archangel…
A towering, stunning woman over two meters tall was standing over Amy, gazing down at her.

blocks.speaker: Archangel Ikael
blocks.content: Who are you? Why are you targeting this child?
Vedium's mask displayed a playful expression, scrunching his eyes cutely and sticking out his tongue to the side.

blocks.speaker: Gunjo Vedium
blocks.content: This is… troublesome.
A single bead of sweat blinked in and out of existence.
